title: Earth fault on a single loop

causes.cause: Water ingress at a detector base or MCP backbox in a wet area
causes.remediation: Walk the loop with a megger from the FIP outwards. Half the loop, retest. Pull the suspect base, dry, replace gasket.

causes.cause: Cable nicked during ceiling works (recent trades on site)
causes.remediation: Check last work order. Inspect cables in the suspected zone for staple / screw damage.

causes.cause: Failed isolator module shorting screen to earth
causes.remediation: Lift each isolator in turn from common, retest earth resistance after each.

causes.cause: FIP earth bond loose or carrying transient from another service
causes.remediation: Verify FIP earth termination torque; check no other equipment is sharing the bond.

title: Loop short circuit (short fault)

causes.cause: Crushed cable inside ceiling void — usually behind a bulkhead or near a recently moved AC unit
causes.remediation: Walk the loop with a megger and TDR. Check known compression points (slab penetrations, bulkheads, light cans). Replace the damaged section.

causes.cause: Two conductors touching inside a junction box with poor terminations
causes.remediation: Open suspected JBs in the order the loop runs. Look for stripped insulation and hot spots.

causes.cause: A failed loop device shorted across +/- internally
causes.remediation: Bisect the loop at the nearest isolator. Whichever half clears the fault contains the failed device. Continue bisecting.

causes.cause: Sounder / strobe wired to loop powered base instead of dedicated SCI
causes.remediation: Verify the device wiring matches the cause-and-effect. Move sounders off the SLC if the protocol forbids it.

title: Charger fault on FIP

causes.cause: One battery in series has failed (open or shorted cell)
causes.remediation: Disconnect batteries, measure each individually under no load then under 2A load. Replace as a matched pair.

causes.cause: Charger fuse blown
causes.remediation: Check inline fuse / PCB fuse on charger output. Replace like-for-like.

causes.cause: Mains supply dropped overnight, charger not yet recovered
causes.remediation: Check mains LED and 230 V at terminals. Allow charge cycle, retest in 4 hours.

causes.cause: Ambient temperature outside charger range
causes.remediation: Many panels derate the charger above 35 °C. Improve cupboard ventilation.

title: Battery load test failed (AS 1851 annual)

causes.cause: Battery beyond design life (typically 4–5 years for VRLA SLA)
causes.remediation: Replace as a matched pair. Document install date on label and in service record.

causes.cause: Bank size undersized for current panel load (devices added after install)
causes.remediation: Re-run battery sizing using current loop loading + extras (mimic, ASE, sounders). If under-sized, upsize or remove load.

causes.cause: Charger not floating at correct voltage (high or low)
causes.remediation: Measure float voltage with a calibrated DMM (~13.6–13.8 V at 25 °C per battery). Adjust or replace charger card.

causes.cause: Brief mains drop during the test invalidating it
causes.remediation: Re-run the test on a stable power day; record start/end times.

title: Intermittent alarm on the same zone

causes.cause: Steam, dust or insects entering one detector
causes.remediation: Identify highest-analogue device on the zone; clean head, fit insect screen if applicable.

causes.cause: Detector at end of life (>10 years on photoelectric)
causes.remediation: Check install date label. Replace per AS 1670.1 / manufacturer recommendation.

causes.cause: Loop voltage sag — too many devices, marginal cable
causes.remediation: Measure loop voltage at far end during alarm; review loop loading.

causes.cause: Day/night sensitivity transition kicking the device over the threshold
causes.remediation: Verify day/night profile. Tighten night threshold or move device to less-disturbed location.

title: Sounder fails to activate on alarm

causes.cause: Cause-and-effect rule missing or output wrongly mapped
causes.remediation: View CBE on panel or in PC tool. Confirm zone-to-output mapping; force the output to verify hardware.

causes.cause: Sounder circuit fuse blown / SCI tripped
causes.remediation: Reset the sounder circuit isolator at the FIP; replace the fuse if open. Look for the short that caused it.

causes.cause: End-of-line resistor missing or wrong value
causes.remediation: Verify EOL value at the far end matches panel spec (often 4k7 or 10k); replace if missing.

causes.cause: Sounder addressed but disabled in last engineer session
causes.remediation: Check disabled list at FIP; clear the disable.

title: Sounder humming / chattering at rest

causes.cause: Polarity reversed on a parallel sounder run
causes.remediation: Check polarity at every device on the SAC; correct the offending leg.

causes.cause: Earth leak feeding the sounder back-EMF
causes.remediation: Resolve loop earth fault first; the sounder noise often clears once earth is solid.

title: ASE fault to brigade

causes.cause: Telstra / NBN line carrier issue at premises
causes.remediation: Test ASE line; raise carrier fault if line unavailable. Don't rebuild ASE first.

causes.cause: ASE polled by monitoring centre showing comm fail
causes.remediation: Call monitoring centre; confirm last successful poll time before site work.

causes.cause: FIP relay output to ASE not pulling — output fuse / config
causes.remediation: Force the brigade output via engineer menu; verify ASE input changes state.

causes.cause: ASE battery flat / charger fault on ASE itself
causes.remediation: Measure ASE battery; replace if below 12.0 V at rest. Check ASE charger output.

title: Monitoring centre reports no test signal at scheduled time

causes.cause: ASE clock drift (time-of-day signal sent but slot missed)
causes.remediation: Resync ASE clock to NTP / monitoring centre. Verify next scheduled signal.

causes.cause: Carrier slow during peak — signal sent but timed-out at MC
causes.remediation: Coordinate with monitoring centre on retransmission policy and timeout window.

causes.cause: Customer ID misconfigured at site or MC
causes.remediation: Verify account / customer ID strings on both ends.

title: Frequent mains failure events on FIP

causes.cause: Sub-board RCD nuisance tripping
causes.remediation: Move FIP supply to a dedicated RCBO labelled 'Fire — do not switch' per AS 3000.

causes.cause: Power supply transformer / PSU failing under load
causes.remediation: Measure PSU output at standby and during alarm. Replace if drops below spec.

causes.cause: Local generator or inverter handover producing brief dropout
causes.remediation: Coordinate with site facilities; tune handover timing or fit a UPS section ahead of FIP.

title: Detector reporting high analogue value (drift / contamination)

causes.cause: Dust accumulation in ceiling void over years
causes.remediation: Vacuum / replace head with clean unit per the panel's drift compensation limits.

causes.cause: Adjacent kitchen / steam exposure
causes.remediation: Relocate detector outside steam path; consider heat detector at the same point.

causes.cause: Detector beyond service life
causes.remediation: Replace per AS 1670.1 manufacturer-recommended life (typically 10 yr photoelectric).

title: Detector showing 'maintenance' or 'service required' flag

causes.cause: Auto-drift compensation has reached its limit
causes.remediation: Clean the head. If flag persists after clean, replace.

causes.cause: Device firmware mismatch after a panel firmware bump
causes.remediation: Verify device versions in the loop status screen; reflash devices via vendor tool if supported.

title: MCP triggering without operation (false alarm)

causes.cause: Glass element loose / cracked from prior tampering
causes.remediation: Replace element; reset MCP. Add tamper sticker.

causes.cause: MCP location subject to physical knocks (loading dock, plant room)
causes.remediation: Fit transparent guard or relocate per AS 1670.1 6.4 mounting guidance.

causes.cause: Internal microswitch failure
causes.remediation: Replace MCP; fault data captured in event log for warranty.

title: Isolator chattering between fault and clear

causes.cause: Marginal short on the loop section the isolator covers
causes.remediation: Investigate the isolated section; commonly a damaged base or a dripping pipe near a device.

causes.cause: Failed isolator module itself
causes.remediation: Bypass the isolator temporarily; if chatter clears, replace it.

title: Lift recall did not occur on alarm test

causes.cause: Wrong zone mapped to lift recall in cause-and-effect
causes.remediation: Verify CBE: lift recall input must trigger from the lift-shaft and lift-lobby smoke detectors (per AS 1735.11).

causes.cause: Lift controller relay not seeing FIP output
causes.remediation: Force the FIP output; meter at lift controller terminal. Replace relay or wire if dead.

causes.cause: Lift recall disabled at lift controller via maintenance switch
causes.remediation: Confirm with lift contractor; reset switch and retest.

title: Smoke curtain / fire door release did not operate

causes.cause: Hold-open device fuse blown / 24 V supply lost
causes.remediation: Verify hold-open supply; replace fuse; check no upstream short.

causes.cause: Output relay not mapped in CBE
causes.remediation: Add the trigger zone to the curtain/door output rule.

title: Networked / sub-panel comms failure

causes.cause: Network cable broken / patched into wrong fibre at IT change
causes.remediation: Verify network port LEDs at both ends; trace to last patch.

causes.cause: Sub-panel address conflict after a node was added
causes.remediation: Inspect node IDs in network config; renumber duplicates.

causes.cause: Different firmware versions on the network bus
causes.remediation: Update all nodes to a common firmware level via vendor tool.

title: WIP / warden phone faulty (no audio or constant fault)

causes.cause: Handset cable damaged at the wall jack
causes.remediation: Replace handset / cord. Test bidirectional audio with a second handset.

causes.cause: EWIS line resistance out of spec from added taps
causes.remediation: Measure line resistance; rebalance the run or split into a second WIP line.

title: Speaker line / SAC fault on EWIS

causes.cause: Open-circuit fault — broken speaker tap or kicked cable
causes.remediation: Use line tester at the SAC to identify break point. Splice or replace cable.

causes.cause: Short-circuit fault — pinched cable or shorted speaker
causes.remediation: Bisect the run; replace the failed speaker.

causes.cause: EOL resistor missing or wrong value
causes.remediation: Verify EOL at far end (commonly 10 kΩ); refit if missing.

title: VESDA / aspirating airflow fault

causes.cause: Sampling pipe blocked / capped during recent works
causes.remediation: Inspect pipework end caps and joins; clear blockage; rebalance.

causes.cause: Filter element exhausted
causes.remediation: Replace filter cartridge per manufacturer schedule (Xtralis VLF/VLP).

causes.cause: Aspirator fan worn / rpm low
causes.remediation: Check fan rpm via VSC/Xtralis tool; replace fan if below threshold.

title: Recurring false alarm overnight, same time window

causes.cause: Cleaner triggering smoke detector with extension pole / spray
causes.remediation: Coordinate with cleaning contractor; consider isolation window overnight (with site approval).

causes.cause: Plant cycling (HVAC / kitchen exhaust) producing smoke ingress
causes.remediation: Reposition detector; consider duct detector instead of point detector.

causes.cause: Day/night threshold not switching on time
causes.remediation: Verify panel time-of-day clock; confirm day/night profile in CBE.

title: Panel keypad locked / engineer login not accepted

causes.cause: Caps / shift state on physical keys causing PIN mismatch
causes.remediation: Verify keypad mode; some panels are case-sensitive on alphanumeric.

causes.cause: Lockout after N failed attempts (vendor-specific)
causes.remediation: Wait the cool-down or power cycle after authorised approval; document attempt.

causes.cause: Engineer code rotated by previous service company
causes.remediation: Use vendor master / dealer process to recover; never store codes in panel labels.

title: Sprinkler flow switch input showing fault / open

causes.cause: Flow switch monitored input EOL missing after recent test
causes.remediation: Refit EOL at flow switch terminals; verify input matches per AS 2118 monitoring.

causes.cause: Wiring trapped during sprinkler maintenance
causes.remediation: Inspect cable run from FIP to flow switch; repair.

title: HVAC duct detector not seeing test smoke during AS 1851 6-monthly

causes.cause: Sampling tube blocked or pointing wrong way in duct
causes.remediation: Reorient sampling tube into airflow; clean tube.

causes.cause: AC fan off during the test — no airflow to push test smoke
causes.remediation: Coordinate with HVAC tech; test with system running.

title: Event log near full / oldest events being overwritten

causes.cause: Persistent fault generating thousands of events per day
causes.remediation: Identify and resolve the chattering fault first; archive log before clearing.

causes.cause: Log rotation not configured
causes.remediation: Print / export the log per AS 1851 record-keeping; clear with engineer authorisation.
